steps.title: Identify the key contradiction
steps.content: The student's claim requires evidence showing people hold contradictory beliefs: they think cultured meat is good but don't want to consume it. This gap between positive assessment and reluctance is what creates a barrier to adoption.

steps.title: Match the contradiction to the choices
steps.content: B demonstrates the required contradiction: consumers surveyed believed cultured meat would benefit the environment but very few were willing to eat it. The others either address unrelated matters (A on packaging, C on production details) or present one-sided support (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
